posts.name: Mattia Campana
posts.text:
Dear Experts,
I am performing an analysis with the signal described by a RooDataHist, while the background is described by a binned PDF. When I try running the ValidateDatacards.py script I get this error:
image1920×302 62.1 KB
You can find an example datacard attached, but the root file with the histogram and the pdf are to heavy to be uploaded here.
How can I solve the issue to proceed with the analysis pre-approval?
Thanks,
Mattia
datacard_std_3par_LQumu_M1000_L1p0.txt (17.4 KB)
posts.cooked_html:
Dear Experts,

I am performing an analysis with the signal described by a RooDataHist, while the background is described by a binned PDF. When I try running the ValidateDatacards.py script I get this error:

image1920×302 62.1 KB

You can find an example datacard attached, but the root file with the histogram and the pdf are to heavy to be uploaded here.

How can I solve the issue to proceed with the analysis pre-approval?

Thanks,

Mattia
datacard_std_3par_LQumu_M1000_L1p0.txt (17.4 KB)

posts.name: Adinda de Wit
posts.text:
Dear Mattia,
ValidateDatacards is mainly intended for histogram(TH1)-based analyses. I thought it exited a bit more gracefully in case of non-TH1-based analyses though - do you know what version of CombineHarvester you are using? (Or alternatively, if you cannot check this, when you checked it out?)
Either way, I think in your case you’ll probably have to skip this step of the automated validation checks.
Best wishes,
Adinda
posts.cooked_html:
Dear Mattia,

ValidateDatacards is mainly intended for histogram(TH1)-based analyses. I thought it exited a bit more gracefully in case of non-TH1-based analyses though - do you know what version of CombineHarvester you are using? (Or alternatively, if you cannot check this, when you checked it out?)

Either way, I think in your case you’ll probably have to skip this step of the automated validation checks.

Best wishes,
Adinda

posts.name: Mattia Campana
posts.text:
Dear Adinda,
thank you for your quick reply, I am using the CombineHarvester master branch and I did the last pull on 2023-05-11.
I will skip this step then.
Best,
Mattia
